title: Tiny Dino Discovery Shakes Up History
story: Roar! A new dinosaur species burst onto the scene in 2013. Scientists in China unearthed bones of Aorun zhaoi, a small but fierce meat-eater from 161 million years ago. This pint-sized predator, a member of the theropod family, lived long before T. rex. The discovery on May 3 helped fill gaps in our understanding of dinosaur evolution, showing how these amazing creatures changed over time.

story: The sky darkened unexpectedly as the Moon blocked the Sun's light. On May 3, 1374 BC, Mesopotamian astronomers in Ugarit witnessed an awe-inspiring solar eclipse. title: Poland's Groundbreaking Constitution
story: A bold leap forward! In 1791, Poland and Lithuania joined forces to create Europe's first modern constitution. The Great Sejm, their parliament, proclaimed this groundbreaking document on May 3. It laid out new rules for how the country should be run, giving more power to the people and setting an example for other nations to follow.
activity_question: What did the Great Sejm proclaim on May 3, 1791?
follow_up_question: Why do you think having a constitution is important for a country?

title: A Royal Gift of Knowledge
story: King Otto of Greece had a big idea. He wanted to bring more learning to his country. So in 1837, on May 3, he founded the University of Athens. This was a huge deal! It became the first modern university in the Eastern Mediterranean region. Students could now get a great education without traveling far from home.
activity_question: What did King Otto of Greece found?
follow_up_question: Why do you think having a university in Greece was important?
